steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Rhetorical Synthesis question. The student wants to emphasize a similarity between first position (Vaganova method) and demi-seconde position (Royal Academy of Dance). Look for the choice that highlights what the two positions share rather than how they differ.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘First position in Vaganova method ballet and demi-seconde position in Royal Academy of Dance ballet are both positions for the dancer's arms.’: This directly states the shared trait — both are arm positions — which is the similarity the student wants to emphasize. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘Both first position and demi-seconde position are positions in Royal Academy of Dance ballet.’: Factually wrong — first position belongs to Vaganova method, not Royal Academy of Dance. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘In first position, both arms are rounded to roughly align the fingers with the navel, in contrast to demi-seconde position, where both arms are out to the sides but angled downward.’: This emphasizes a contrast between the two positions, not a similarity. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘In Vaganova method ballet, there are a number of positions, including first position.’: This describes only the Vaganova method and says nothing about demi-seconde position; no similarity is drawn. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
